query: I just took a screenshot of my Framer canvas — can you tell me if the design looks professional? the file is at ~/Desktop/homepage-v3.png
rate this layout: dark bg, 3 column feature grid, hero with gradient, Inter font everywhere. does it pass the anti-slop checklist?
what's wrong with this design? I've got #FFFFFF text on #1A1A1A, mixed border radius (some 8px some 16px), and my body text is set to 13px.
forge-review my current canvas state. I want to know the grade before I publish.
does this hero section look good? purple to pink gradient background, centered text spanning full width, stock office photo below. heading is 42px Inter.
evaluate my color palette — I'm using #0A0A0A base, #141414 for cards, #4F46E5 for CTAs, #E8E8E8 for text. Dark mode SaaS landing page.
check the contrast ratios on my design. heading is Space Grotesk #E8E8E8 on #0A0A0A, subhead is DM Sans #888888 on #0A0A0A, CTA is white on #4F46E5.
is my spacing consistent? I'm using 32px padding inside cards, 24px gaps between them, 96px between sections, 16px for button padding. Everything on 8px grid?
I finished building my landing page in Framer. Before I publish, can you do a full visual QA pass? Grade each dimension and tell me what to fix.
grade this design against the comparison rubric — I want to know if it's A-tier (ship it) or if there are issues holding it back.
can you review my pull request? it's PR #42 on our github repo. Changes are in src/components/Dashboard.tsx and adds a new chart widget.
write me a Python script that resizes images in a folder to 800x600px. Use Pillow and preserve aspect ratio with padding.
help me optimize this SQL query — it's doing a full table scan on the orders table which has 2M rows. Here's the EXPLAIN output...
I need to add dark mode support to my React app. I'm using Tailwind and want to respect the user's system preference.
set up ESLint and Prettier for my TypeScript project. I want strict rules, no semicolons, single quotes, and 2-space indent.
review my API endpoint for security vulnerabilities — it takes user input and runs a database query. I'm worried about SQL injection.
can you explain the difference between server components and client components in Next.js? I keep getting hydration errors.
help me write a Dockerfile for my Node.js app. It uses pnpm, needs to build the frontend, and should run on port 3000.
I want to A/B test two different hero sections on my marketing site. How do I set up feature flags with LaunchDarkly?
look at this error in my terminal — npm install is failing with ERESOLVE and peer dependency conflicts between react 18 and some old library.
